behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real STT model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation). A mock STT response would hide this class of failure. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets STT output preservation itself, not a multi-agent decision chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript chunks retain correct speaker labels for known utterances used later for attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic over transcript text to structured fields; the failure boundary is local extraction logic, not OS/network/LLM. Rejected Integration because wording does not require cross-module state handoff beyond one parser transformation.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript fixtures with explicit assignments, test parser function returns exact assignee/task tuples in the summary input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summary decisions (coverage and attribution of action items). Stubbed model outputs would trivially pass and miss omissions/hallucinated coverage failures. Rejected Workflow because this behavior can be validated from a prepared transcript into one real summarization step.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcripts with known per-attendee assignments to the real summarization model and assert final summary includes at least one action item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is a module-handoff/schema-routing behavior: identified decisions must land in the correct output section across component boundaries. No real OS/network required, and no dependence on real model judgment if decisions are already identified. Rejected Agentic due behavior text: "identified decisions" and "routes ... into schema" disqualify model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor output into summary schema builder in-process and assert identified decision objects appear only under `decisions`, not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the live model output captures and places decision content correctly; that is model-output quality dependent. Mocked summaries would hide real misses. Rejected Workflow because one real summarization decision stage over transcript is sufficient to expose this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts with known decision statements, run real summarization, and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section and absent as only narrative mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly sequential multi-step processing with model-dependent outputs across segments and merge coherence; failures emerge across chained agentic stages (segment STT/summarization + merge). Mocks can miss drift/duplication/omission introduced across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation flow, verify sequential segment processing occurred, and validate merged final summary is coherent and preserves cross-segment tasks/decisions.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is end-to-end pipeline completion over real recording with multiple model-involved stages and external infrastructure. Minimum failure boundary is full workflow orchestration, not a single module or call.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like E2E run: upload real meeting audio, run real transcription and summarization, and assert structured output is delivered automatically with required sections populated.
